summary: Soundboard capture with spacious yet slightly brittle ’71 sound; show features a standout Other One and solid Dark Star.
review: The recording is a sound‑board source that delivers an unusually open, spacious feel for the era, though some listeners note a hard‑edged, brittle tone lacking later‑year warmth. Musically the concert is “fairly good,” highlighted by an excellent The Other One and a nicely flowing Dark Star, with an ambitious (if imperfect) attempt at the full That's It suite.

ai_rating.rationale: Both reviewers rate the set highly (5 and 4 stars). The performance is praised for specific jams, while the recording is described as good but a bit thin‑sounding; combined weighting yields an overall ~4‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Spacious and open sound typical of early ’71 Fillmore recordings, but described as hard‑edged and brittle with less warmth than later years; no major dropouts or distortion reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Overall a fairly good show that attempts the full That's It suite; the intimate Fillmore venue contributes to an open feel, though Cryptical Envelopment (part 1) was deemed not up to release quality.
